title: Black Eyed Please

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Classic Simpsons opening sequence setup
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase that defines the character - perfect integration and economy, though not original within the show's context

jokes.text: You little!
jokes.setup: Homer chasing Bart in opening sequence
jokes.punchline: Classic interrupted threat as Homer fails to catch Bart
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer frustration, good economy but predictable setup

jokes.text: Bathroom break! I spilled my pills! Music today has no melody! Everybody out!
jokes.setup: Retirement home chaos
jokes.punchline: Random complaints escalating to evacuation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from mundane to absurd complaints

jokes.text: Children, you can stop writing letters of support to our troops; that was just busywork.
jokes.setup: Principal addressing students about troop support
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of what seemed meaningful as mere busywork
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical edge about school meaninglessness, perfectly delivered with Skinner's bureaucratic tone

jokes.text: Not to worry, she has been treated with heavy doses of... Lorazepam and other drugs from the... Benzodiazepine family.
jokes.setup: Explaining teacher's absence to children
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately detailed medical information about prescription drugs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of how adults talk to children - way too much medical detail delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Lisa, without corporate sponsorship, we wouldn't be able to teach you the three R's: reading, refreshment and Raspberry Buzz Lite. Get your razz on!
jokes.setup: Lisa hoping for non-corporate education
jokes.punchline: Skinner's blatant corporate corruption of basic education
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of corporate influence in schools, good twist on 'three R's' with absurd product placement

jokes.text: She's brought her own orrery! And it's got no Pluto! How cutting edge.
jokes.setup: Lisa excited about new teacher's astronomical equipment
jokes.punchline: Nerdy enthusiasm about Pluto's demotion from planet status
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - only she would be excited about accurate astronomical models

jokes.text: Do not call her by the obvious dirty nickname. Now I must meet with the new art teacher, Mr. Testacleese.
jokes.setup: Warning against inappropriate nicknames
jokes.punchline: Immediately mentions teacher with obvious dirty name
jokes.explanation: Great setup-payoff structure with immediate contradiction of what he just said

jokes.text: Get back in your seat. If I have any apples that need polishing, I'll let you know.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to welcome new teacher enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Teacher immediately shutting down Lisa with sarcastic apple-polishing reference
jokes.explanation: Establishes the character dynamic well, though apple-polishing reference is somewhat predictable

jokes.text: If I can't remember the right letter, I just put an 'A.'
jokes.setup: Ralph discussing homework strategy
jokes.punchline: Completely illogical approach to spelling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph logic - completely wrong but delivered with innocent confidence

jokes.text: 'B.' As in, 'because I say so.'
jokes.setup: Lisa expecting good grade on assignment
jokes.punchline: Arbitrary grading with authoritarian explanation
jokes.explanation: Great economy in showing teacher's arbitrary authority, perfect character establishment

jokes.text: I thought you might be cold, honey, so I put this chinchilla coat on you. Fur?! It's not really chinchilla. I just thought she needed more sleep.
jokes.setup: Marge seemingly putting fur coat on Lisa
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually the cat and misunderstands Lisa's reaction
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Marge's characteristic misunderstanding of the situation

jokes.text: Hey, hey, Homer J. How many beers did you kill today?
jokes.setup: Meeting Ned's parents
jokes.punchline: Ned's father encouraging Homer's drinking, opposite of Ned
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast showing where Ned gets his disapproval from by showing the opposite

jokes.text: Son, you are the Mayor of Dullsville.
jokes.setup: Ned trying to maintain family decorum
jokes.punchline: Father calling him mayor of a made-up boring place
jokes.explanation: Decent insult with good economy, establishes family dynamic

jokes.text: Hello, police? I'd like to report a buzz-kill in progress. There's no emergency here. And please charge us for the false report.
jokes.setup: Family tension at breakfast
jokes.punchline: Mother calling police on her own son, then Ned apologizing for the call
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of family dysfunction with Ned's characteristic over-politeness

jokes.text: I'm hop-scotching from paragraph to paragraph, climbing the monkey bars of plot and theme.
jokes.setup: Teacher telling Lisa to have free play
jokes.punchline: Lisa describing reading as playground activities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor that perfectly captures Lisa's intellectual nature and love of learning

jokes.text: Please keep them together. If not for me, for the mother.
jokes.setup: Teacher separating Lisa's stuffed animals
jokes.punchline: Lisa's overly dramatic plea about keeping toys together
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Lisa's dramatic tendencies

jokes.text: Six miles, and I'm all smiles.
jokes.setup: Ned's mother returning from power walk
jokes.punchline: Rhyming catchphrase about exercise
jokes.explanation: Simple rhyme with decent economy but lacks originality

jokes.text: Look at the size of that bird. I'd go Henry VIII on those drumsticks.
jokes.setup: Homer noticing a large bird
jokes.punchline: Historical reference to excessive eating
jokes.explanation: Good historical reference that fits Homer's gluttonous character

jokes.text: Homer, I can't believe you're partaking with my parents. Yeah, it's medicinal. We had a pain in our neck.
jokes.setup: Ned surprised Homer is smoking with his parents
jokes.punchline: Homer's pun about Ned being a 'pain in the neck'
jokes.explanation: Solid pun with good setup, though not particularly original

jokes.text: But the storytelling is finally catching up to the technology.
jokes.setup: Doctor saying Homer may never see 3-D films again
jokes.punchline: Homer's optimistic take on missing mediocre 3-D movies
jokes.explanation: Good satirical observation about 3-D movie quality, unexpectedly insightful for Homer

jokes.text: My mustache fell out; this is a clip-on.
jokes.setup: Ned explaining his guilt about hitting Homer
jokes.punchline: Reveals his signature mustache is fake due to stress
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character detail that subverts Ned's wholesome image while being perfectly economical

jokes.text: What about Christian Archie comics? Neither canonical nor comical.
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting religious comic books for prayer
jokes.punchline: Ned's dismissal using religious and comedy terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'canonical' that works both religiously and literarily

jokes.text: Hail Superman, wearing tights, Clark Kent be thy name, one nation, under Zod...
jokes.setup: Homer creating a prayer to Superman
jokes.punchline: Mixing Superman lore with traditional prayer format
jokes.explanation: Creative mashup of religious prayer with Superman mythology, well-structured parody

jokes.text: I never made that noise in my life!
jokes.setup: Homer asking for Marge's 'famous murmur'
jokes.punchline: Marge's indignant denial of making such sounds
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's dignity, though setup is somewhat unclear

jokes.text: Thanks, Flanders. Punch my eye, destroy my marriage. Now just pull the plug and let me die! That's the plug for the light.
jokes.setup: Homer's dramatic death wish
jokes.punchline: Marge's literal interpretation about electrical plug
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Marge's practical response to Homer's dramatics

jokes.text: Please don't use that word in bed.
jokes.setup: Maude saying 'coldcocking'
jokes.punchline: Ned's prudish reaction to innocent word
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned character moment showing his excessive prudishness

jokes.text: Hey-diddly hell-diddly, Welcome neighborino
jokes.setup: Ned's nightmare/drug-induced vision
jokes.punchline: Hell version of Ned using his speech patterns with 'hell'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character subversion maintaining Ned's speech patterns in hellish context

jokes.text: Down here, we worship famous atheist Richard Dawkins, author of The God Delusion!
jokes.setup: Hell scene with Ned
jokes.punchline: Hell worshipping atheist author instead of Satan
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical take on religious fears and contemporary atheism debates

jokes.text: I'm making Catholic saint stew.
jokes.setup: Hell version of domestic scene
jokes.punchline: Marge cooking religious figures
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor maintaining Marge's domestic character in hellish context

jokes.text: More than I want unicorns to be real.
jokes.setup: Lisa expressing how much she wants to know something
jokes.punchline: Using impossible wish as comparison
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa character moment with childlike comparison

jokes.text: Look, kids can be cruel, Dick. Ha-ha! We don't know when we've gone too far!
jokes.setup: Skinner consoling teacher with unfortunate name
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrating cruelty by laughing at the name
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic structure where Skinner immediately proves his own point about cruelty

jokes.text: Look who's on the Ferris wheel. Your precious joeys!
jokes.setup: Math problem about Ferris wheel
jokes.punchline: Teacher using Lisa's stuffed animals as threat in word problem
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the teacher's psychological warfare on Lisa

jokes.text: Here's the new deal: you fail!
jokes.setup: Social studies lesson about New Deal
jokes.punchline: Teacher twisting historical reference into threat
jokes.explanation: Clever pun that works both historically and as a threat, good economy

jokes.text: What? This isn't Sweden? And I'm not King Olaf? Oh, I got some explaining to do down at the bank.
jokes.setup: Confusion about metric system
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's complete geographical and royal confusion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of confusion that's perfectly in character for Grandpa

jokes.text: Quiet, nerd!
jokes.setup: Skinner denying school hires bullies
jokes.punchline: Immediately bullying someone
jokes.explanation: Good ironic structure showing Skinner's hypocrisy immediately

jokes.text: There's this guy at my plant, caused three meltdowns, and he still keeps his job. Homer, that's you.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about incompetent coworker
jokes.punchline: Marge revealing it's Homer himself
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer lack of self-awareness perfectly executed

jokes.text: Jeepers creepers, pop my peeper!
jokes.setup: Ned wanting Homer to hit him
jokes.punchline: Childish rhyming request for violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned-ism with innocent language for violent request

jokes.text: I'm insisting on a fisting.
jokes.setup: Ned wanting to be punched
jokes.punchline: Unfortunate word choice with sexual connotations
jokes.explanation: Relies on double entendre, somewhat cheap but fits Ned's innocence

jokes.text: Better man, coming through! Way better-better-better! Way better-better-better!
jokes.setup: Homer discovering he's the 'better man'
jokes.punchline: Obnoxious celebration of moral superiority
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's ego even in virtue

jokes.text: I didn't know you were left-handed.
jokes.setup: Ned being punched by Homer's wife
jokes.punchline: Same line from when Homer got punched
jokes.explanation: Good callback that creates symmetry and completes the cycle

jokes.text: I didn't know you were left-handed.
jokes.setup: Marge punching Ned
jokes.punchline: Casual observation after violent act
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Marge's matter-of-fact delivery after violence

jokes.text: Every rap sheet has a first line.
jokes.setup: Lisa in detention for first time
jokes.punchline: Philosophical acceptance of criminal progression
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa adapting to new situation with intelligence

jokes.text: What are you in for, murdering Chopin?
jokes.setup: Asking Lisa what she did wrong
jokes.punchline: Assumption of intellectual crime fitting her character
jokes.explanation: Creative joke about what kind of crime an intellectual would commit

jokes.text: That means all of us can be teachers. Boo-yah! I'm gonna buy me a Hyundai Elantra!
jokes.setup: Learning teachers can be bullies
jokes.punchline: Illogical conclusion leading to specific car purchase
jokes.explanation: Absurd logic leap with oddly specific car choice

jokes.text: I saw the whole thing! That innocent fly flew from that fresh pile of dog feces right into that monster's mouth!
jokes.setup: Panic over Santa's Little Helper eating fly
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic description of natural dog behavior
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy setup though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Never get into Heaven. Took harp lessons for nothing.
jokes.setup: Ned being dragged by dog
jokes.punchline: Assuming he's going to hell, worried about wasted harp practice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Ned's religious anxiety and practical concerns about afterlife preparation

jokes.text: How much plutonium do you want? Got to warn you, it might take me 20 minutes to get it.
jokes.setup: Asking about the 'nuclear option'
jokes.punchline: Ned's literal interpretation and casual access to plutonium
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment with Ned's innocent literalism about nuclear material

jokes.text: Right. I can't be around him 'cause he's so delicious.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she can't date student
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate description of attraction to minor
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable humor that relies on inappropriate teacher-student dynamic

jokes.text: You two are the worst-dressed gay men I've ever met.
jokes.setup: Skinner and Chalmers trying to help
jokes.punchline: Mistaking them for gay couple based on appearance
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor though relies on stereotypes

jokes.text: She hates me because I'm pretty!
jokes.setup: Lisa discovering reason for teacher's dislike
jokes.punchline: Unexpected vanity from usually modest character
jokes.explanation: Good character subversion showing Lisa's rare moment of vanity

jokes.text: She hates me because I'm pretty!
jokes.setup: Lisa learning why teacher dislikes her
jokes.punchline: Lisa's shallow takeaway from complex explanation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Lisa missing deeper point about privilege

tmdb_overview: When Flanders' parents prove to prefer Homer's company to his, he jealously responds by giving Homer a shiner, and then tries desperately to reconcile with him. Meanwhile, Lisa is at a loss to understand why a new substitute teacher is bullying her for no obvious reason.
